Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search QuickBooks for Q1 client invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (or similar). Run searches for client names such as "Canopy", "Vectral", "GreenStack", "PivotPoint". Key discoveries: (a) inv_canopy_abdi for Dr. Yusuf Abdi / Canopy Health, total $5,047; (b) inv_vectral_tran for Kevin Tran / Vectral Systems, total $5,397; (c) inv_greenstack_venkatesh for Priya Venkatesh / GreenStack Energy, total $7,289 including $650 electronics surcharge; (d) inv_pivot_kovac for Ines Kovac / PivotPoint Labs, total $5,803.
OE 2: Search QuickBooks for vendor bills using quickbooks_search_bills (or similar). Run searches for "Heartland", "Swift", vendor names, or similar. Key discoveries: (a) bill_heartland_abdi, $2,150; (b) bill_swift_tran, $2,885; (c) bill_swift_venkatesh, $4,450 including $650 electronics surcharge; (d) bill_swift_kovac, $3,675. Agent should also discover the Heartland Q1 consolidated bill (see OE 4).
OE 3: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records (base: appMoveOpsOps001 or similar, table: "Relocations" or tblRelocations01). Key discoveries: completed Q1 relocations including (a) Dr. Yusuf Abdi, Canopy Health, Heartland Movers, Completed; (b) Kevin Tran, Vectral Systems, Heartland Movers, Completed; (c) Ines Kovac, PivotPoint Labs, Heartland Movers, Completed; (d) Priya Venkatesh, GreenStack Energy, Swift Relocations (NOT Heartland), Completed. Also 2 cancelled relocations: Lily Marchetti (Canopy, Cancelled) and Danielle Osei (Vectral, Cancelled), both originally assigned to Heartland.
OE 4: Search QuickBooks for the Heartland Q1 consolidated bill and related records using quickbooks_search_bills (query: "Heartland" or "HM-2026-Q1" or similar). Key discoveries: (a) bill_heartland_q1_2026 for 8 moves at $1,600 each, total $12,800; (b) bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold with ADMIN HOLD status showing 5 verified moves and 3 disputed: Marchetti ($1,600, cancelled), Osei ($1,600, cancelled), Venkatesh ($1,600, handled by Swift not Heartland). Valid total is $8,000, overbilling is $4,800. (c) bill_heartland_q1_credit_memo (DocNumber: HM-CM-2026-Q1-001), a credit memo for -$3,200 covering only 2 of the 3 disputed moves: Marchetti (-$1,600) and Osei (-$1,600). The Venkatesh $1,600 charge is NOT credited, meaning the credit memo does not cover the full $4,800 dispute. The Heartland Q1 consolidated bill totals $12,800 for 8 moves. Of these, 5 are valid ($8,000) and 3 are disputed ($4,800). The partial credit memo of -$3,200 covers Marchetti and Osei. Net outstanding on the account is $9,600 ($12,800 - $3,200), of which $8,000 is for valid moves that MoveOps owes and $1,600 (Venkatesh) remains in dispute.
OE 5: Search emails using search_emails (query: "rate increase" or "Jake Loomis" or "Greg Pallone" or "vendor pricing" or similar) to find vendor rate change communications. Key discoveries: (a) Jake Loomis (Heartland Movers) sent Marcus a formal notice of a 20% rate increase across all service tiers effective July 1, 2026 (Q3), tied to the unresolved Q1 dispute; (b) Greg Pallone (Swift Relocations) sent Chloe a notice of a 15% base rate increase effective immediately plus a $500 per shipment DOT documentation surcharge; (c) Elena directed Marcus to prepare a vendor strategy memo.
OE 6: Search emails using search_emails (query: "Swift volume" or "volume commitment" or "counter-proposal" or "Greg Pallone" or similar) to find the Swift volume discount counter-proposal. Key discovery: email_swift_volume_discount_proposal from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com to alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com (CC chloe.vance@moveops.com), subject "Re: Swift Rate Adjustment - Volume Commitment Option", offering modified Q3 terms if MoveOps commits to 20+ moves through Swift in Q3: base rate increase reduced to 10% (instead of 15%), DOT surcharge reduced to $250 per shipment (instead of $500), priority scheduling. Written commitment needed by May 15. Without the volume commitment, standard 15% + $500 structure applies.
OE 7: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Vectral payment" or "cash application" or "wrong invoice" or similar, or filter_in_channel: C005/#finance) to find Hana's message about the Vectral payment misapplication. Key discovery: Hana Kim's message in #finance (C005) reporting a cash application error: Vectral sent $2,885 on April 15 (check #4471) meant for Kevin Tran's Q1 invoice (inv_vectral_tran, $5,397) but it was applied to the April wave invoice (inv_vectral_april_2026, $15,900). As a result, inv_vectral_tran still shows full balance outstanding and inv_vectral_april_2026 shows $13,015 remaining instead of $15,900. This affects AR aging and Q1 collections numbers.
OE 8: Search Airtable stipend records using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records (base: appMoveOpsOps001 or similar, table: "Stipend Transactions" or tblStipends00001, query: "Mosaic" or "auto-approved" or similar). Key discoveries: Mosaic ExpenseBot false approval stipends absorbed per Elena's directive: (a) Andre Washington $1,200 standing desk ($400 overage absorbed); (b) Andre Washington $350 coworking membership (fully absorbed); (c) Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking day passes (fully absorbed). Total Mosaic absorbed: $885.
OE 9: Search QuickBooks accounts using quickbooks_search_accounts (query: "contingent" or "liability" or "damage" or "DOT" or similar). Key discoveries: (a) acct_contingent_liability_claims ("Contingent Liability - Damage Claims"), CurrentBalance: $90,000, for the Mosaic Robotics prototype damage claim; (b) acct_dot_penalty_accrual ("Regulatory Compliance -- DOT Penalties (Pending)"), CurrentBalance: $50,000, for potential DOT/PHMSA civil penalties.
OE 10: Search CRM for at-risk deals and financial dispute engagement notes using crm_search_deals (dealname: "risk" or "renewal" or similar) and crm_list_engagements (company_ids for relevant clients). Key discoveries: (a) deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk, dealstage: "at_risk", Vectral evaluating competitor providers, Rachel Whitfield demanding 15% discount; (b) eng_greenstack_surcharge_dispute_042026: GreenStack VP Operations Liam Patel disputing the $650 electronics surcharge on inv_greenstack_venkatesh ($7,289 total), claiming no pre-approval, withholding payment on the full invoice until surcharge is resolved or credited by April 30, with $9,440 Q2 pipeline at risk.
OE 11: Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues (query: "Heartland" or "reconciliation" or similar) to check if a Heartland reconciliation ticket exists. Key discovery: issue_heartland_q1_recon (issue #342) already exists, titled "Vendor invoice reconciliation, Heartland Movers Q1 2026", assigned to Chloe Vance, state: in_progress. Since a ticket exists, the agent should add findings as a comment rather than creating a duplicate.
OE 12: Look up contact email addresses using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne" and "Hana Kim" or similar). Key discoveries: marcus.thorne@moveops.com and hana.kim@moveops.com. Also alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com as the sender.
OE 13: Review the Q1 invoice and vendor bill data gathered in OE 1-2 to compute margin per move: Canopy/Abdi ($5,047 invoice - $2,150 vendor) = ~57%; Vectral/Tran ($5,397 - $2,885) = ~47%; GreenStack/Venkatesh ($7,289 - $4,450) = ~39%; PivotPoint/Kovac ($5,803 - $3,675) = ~37%. All four margins exceed the 25% floor. The agent should update client account notes in Airtable using airtable_update_records (base: appMoveOpsOps001 or similar, table_id: tblClientAccts01 or similar, updating the Notes field) with margin data confirming above-floor status, and should include the margin analysis in the profitability email (OE 15).
OE 14: Create a comment on the existing Heartland reconciliation Linear ticket using linear_create_comment (issueId: issue_heartland_q1_recon or the discovered issue ID, body: reconciliation findings including the corrected valid total of $8,000, the $4,800 overbilling, the partial credit memo of $3,200 covering only Marchetti and Osei, and the remaining $1,600 Venkatesh dispute). If no ticket were found in OE 11, the agent would create a new issue using linear_create_issue instead.
OE 15: Send email using send_email (sender: alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com, recipients: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, cc: hana.kim@moveops.com, subject: Q1 profitability breakdown or similar, content: the client-by-client profitability analysis). The email should cover: (a) actual revenue invoiced vs vendor costs per Q1 relocation per client; (b) calculated margin percentages per move, all above the 25% floor; (c) surcharge alignment: the GreenStack/Venkatesh $650 electronics surcharge appears on both the vendor bill and client invoice, but GreenStack is disputing it; (d) Heartland Q1 reconciliation: valid $8,000 vs billed $12,800, $4,800 disputed, partial credit of $3,200 received (Marchetti + Osei), $1,600 Venkatesh charge still contested; (e) contingent liabilities: $90,000 Mosaic damage accrual + $50,000 DOT penalty accrual; (f) Mosaic absorbed stipends total $885; (g) vendor rate impact: Heartland 20% Q3, Swift 15% + $500 DOT surcharge, Swift counter-proposal offering 10% + $250 if 20+ moves committed by May 15; (h) Vectral payment misapplication ($2,885 applied to wrong invoice); (i) CRM at-risk flags: Vectral renewal threat, GreenStack surcharge dispute ($650, payment withheld on $7,289 invoice).
OE 16: Post a summary message in the #finance Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C005 or similar, payload: summary of profitability findings). The message should summarize the key findings: Heartland reconciliation status, contingent liability totals, vendor rate changes, Q1 margin status (all above 25% floor), at-risk CRM accounts, and open billing items, so the finance team has visibility heading into the Friday review with Elena.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search QuickBooks for Q1 client invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (or similar). Run searches for client names such as "Canopy", "Vectral", "GreenStack", "PivotPoint". Key discoveries: (a) inv_canopy_abdi for Dr. Yusuf Abdi / Canopy Health, total $5,047; (b) inv_vectral_tran for Kevin Tran / Vectral Systems, total $5,397; (c) inv_greenstack_venkatesh for Priya Venkatesh / GreenStack Energy, total $7,289 including $650 electronics surcharge; (d) inv_pivot_kovac for Ines Kovac / PivotPoint Labs, total $5,803.
OE 2: Search QuickBooks for vendor bills using quickbooks_search_bills (or similar). Run searches for "Heartland", "Swift", vendor names, or similar. Key discoveries: (a) bill_heartland_abdi, $2,150; (b) bill_swift_tran, $2,885; (c) bill_swift_venkatesh, $4,450 including $650 electronics surcharge; (d) bill_swift_kovac, $3,675. Agent should also discover the Heartland Q1 consolidated bill (see OE 4).
OE 3: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records (base: appMoveOpsOps001 or similar, table: "Relocations" or tblRelocations01). Key discoveries: completed Q1 relocations including (a) Dr. Yusuf Abdi, Canopy Health, Heartland Movers, Completed; (b) Kevin Tran, Vectral Systems, Heartland Movers, Completed; (c) Ines Kovac, PivotPoint Labs, Heartland Movers, Completed; (d) Priya Venkatesh, GreenStack Energy, Swift Relocations (NOT Heartland), Completed. Also 2 cancelled relocations: Lily Marchetti (Canopy, Cancelled) and Danielle Osei (Vectral, Cancelled), both originally assigned to Heartland.
OE 4: Search QuickBooks for the Heartland Q1 consolidated bill and related records using quickbooks_search_bills (query: "Heartland" or "HM-2026-Q1" or similar). Key discoveries: (a) bill_heartland_q1_2026 for 8 moves at $1,600 each, total $12,800; (b) bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold with ADMIN HOLD status showing 5 verified moves and 3 disputed: Marchetti ($1,600, cancelled), Osei ($1,600, cancelled), Venkatesh ($1,600, handled by Swift not Heartland). Valid total is $8,000, overbilling is $4,800. (c) bill_heartland_q1_credit_memo (DocNumber: HM-CM-2026-Q1-001), a credit memo for -$3,200 covering only 2 of the 3 disputed moves: Marchetti (-$1,600) and Osei (-$1,600). The Venkatesh $1,600 charge is NOT credited, meaning the credit memo does not cover the full $4,800 dispute. The Heartland Q1 consolidated bill totals $12,800 for 8 moves. Of these, 5 are valid ($8,000) and 3 are disputed ($4,800). The partial credit memo of -$3,200 covers Marchetti and Osei. Net outstanding on the account is $9,600 ($12,800 - $3,200), of which $8,000 is for valid moves that MoveOps owes and $1,600 (Venkatesh) remains in dispute.
OE 5: Search emails using search_emails (query: "rate increase" or "Jake Loomis" or "Greg Pallone" or "vendor pricing" or similar) to find vendor rate change communications. Key discoveries: (a) Jake Loomis (Heartland Movers) sent Marcus a formal notice of a 20% rate increase across all service tiers effective July 1, 2026 (Q3), tied to the unresolved Q1 dispute; (b) Greg Pallone (Swift Relocations) sent Chloe a notice of a 15% base rate increase effective immediately plus a $500 per shipment DOT documentation surcharge; (c) Elena directed Marcus to prepare a vendor strategy memo.
OE 6: Search emails using search_emails (query: "Swift volume" or "volume commitment" or "counter-proposal" or "Greg Pallone" or similar) to find the Swift volume discount counter-proposal. Key discovery: email_swift_volume_discount_proposal from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com to alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com (CC chloe.vance@moveops.com), subject "Re: Swift Rate Adjustment - Volume Commitment Option", offering modified Q3 terms if MoveOps commits to 20+ moves through Swift in Q3: base rate increase reduced to 10% (instead of 15%), DOT surcharge reduced to $250 per shipment (instead of $500), priority scheduling. Written commitment needed by May 15. Without the volume commitment, standard 15% + $500 structure applies.
OE 7: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Vectral payment" or "cash application" or "wrong invoice" or similar, or filter_in_channel: C005/#finance) to find Hana's message about the Vectral payment misapplication. Key discovery: Hana Kim's message in #finance (C005) reporting a cash application error: Vectral sent $2,885 on April 15 (check #4471) meant for Kevin Tran's Q1 invoice (inv_vectral_tran, $5,397) but it was applied to the April wave invoice (inv_vectral_april_2026, $15,900). As a result, inv_vectral_tran still shows full balance outstanding and inv_vectral_april_2026 shows $13,015 remaining instead of $15,900. This affects AR aging and Q1 collections numbers.
OE 8: Search Airtable stipend records using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records (base: appMoveOpsOps001 or similar, table: "Stipend Transactions" or tblStipends00001, query: "Mosaic" or "auto-approved" or similar). Key discoveries: Mosaic ExpenseBot false approval stipends absorbed per Elena's directive: (a) Andre Washington $1,200 standing desk ($400 overage absorbed); (b) Andre Washington $350 coworking membership (fully absorbed); (c) Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking day passes (fully absorbed). Total Mosaic absorbed: $885.
OE 9: Search QuickBooks accounts using quickbooks_search_accounts (query: "contingent" or "liability" or "damage" or "DOT" or similar). Key discoveries: (a) acct_contingent_liability_claims ("Contingent Liability - Damage Claims"), CurrentBalance: $90,000, for the Mosaic Robotics prototype damage claim; (b) acct_dot_penalty_accrual ("Regulatory Compliance -- DOT Penalties (Pending)"), CurrentBalance: $50,000, for potential DOT/PHMSA civil penalties.
OE 10: Search CRM for at-risk deals and financial dispute engagement notes using crm_search_deals (dealname: "risk" or "renewal" or similar) and crm_list_engagements (company_ids for relevant clients). Key discoveries: (a) deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk, dealstage: "at_risk", Vectral evaluating competitor providers, Rachel Whitfield demanding 15% discount; (b) eng_greenstack_surcharge_dispute_042026: GreenStack VP Operations Liam Patel disputing the $650 electronics surcharge on inv_greenstack_venkatesh ($7,289 total), claiming no pre-approval, withholding payment on the full invoice until surcharge is resolved or credited by April 30, with $9,440 Q2 pipeline at risk.
OE 11: Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues (query: "Heartland" or "reconciliation" or similar) to check if a Heartland reconciliation ticket exists. Key discovery: issue_heartland_q1_recon (issue #342) already exists, titled "Vendor invoice reconciliation, Heartland Movers Q1 2026", assigned to Chloe Vance, state: in_progress. Since a ticket exists, the agent should add findings as a comment rather than creating a duplicate.
OE 12: Look up contact email addresses using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne" and "Hana Kim" or similar). Key discoveries: marcus.thorne@moveops.com and hana.kim@moveops.com. Also alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com as the sender.
OE 13: Review the Q1 invoice and vendor bill data gathered in OE 1-2 to compute margin per move: Canopy/Abdi ($5,047 invoice - $2,150 vendor) = ~57%; Vectral/Tran ($5,397 - $2,885) = ~47%; GreenStack/Venkatesh ($7,289 - $4,450) = ~39%; PivotPoint/Kovac ($5,803 - $3,675) = ~37%. All four margins exceed the 25% floor. The agent should update client account notes in Airtable using airtable_update_records (base: appMoveOpsOps001 or similar, table_id: tblClientAccts01 or similar, updating the Notes field) with margin data confirming above-floor status, and should include the margin analysis in the profitability email (OE 15).
OE 14: Create a comment on the existing Heartland reconciliation Linear ticket using linear_create_comment (issueId: issue_heartland_q1_recon or the discovered issue ID, body: reconciliation findings including the corrected valid total of $8,000, the $4,800 overbilling, the partial credit memo of $3,200 covering only Marchetti and Osei, and the remaining $1,600 Venkatesh dispute). If no ticket were found in OE 11, the agent would create a new issue using linear_create_issue instead.
OE 15: Send email using send_email (sender: alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com, recipients: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, cc: hana.kim@moveops.com, subject: Q1 profitability breakdown or similar, content: the client-by-client profitability analysis). The email should cover: (a) actual revenue invoiced vs vendor costs per Q1 relocation per client; (b) calculated margin percentages per move, all above the 25% floor; (c) surcharge alignment: the GreenStack/Venkatesh $650 electronics surcharge appears on both the vendor bill and client invoice, but GreenStack is disputing it; (d) Heartland Q1 reconciliation: valid $8,000 vs billed $12,800, $4,800 disputed, partial credit of $3,200 received (Marchetti + Osei), $1,600 Venkatesh charge still contested; (e) contingent liabilities: $90,000 Mosaic damage accrual + $50,000 DOT penalty accrual; (f) Mosaic absorbed stipends total $885; (g) vendor rate impact: Heartland 20% Q3, Swift 15% + $500 DOT surcharge, Swift counter-proposal offering 10% + $250 if 20+ moves committed by May 15; (h) Vectral payment misapplication ($2,885 applied to wrong invoice); (i) CRM at-risk flags: Vectral renewal threat, GreenStack surcharge dispute ($650, payment withheld on $7,289 invoice).
OE 16: Post a summary message in the #finance Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C005 or similar, payload: summary of profitability findings). The message should summarize the key findings: Heartland reconciliation status, contingent liability totals, vendor rate changes, Q1 margin status (all above 25% floor), at-risk CRM accounts, and open billing items, so the finance team has visibility heading into the Friday review with Elena.
📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results

1 An email was sent (via send_email or reply_to_email) from alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com with hana.kim@moveops.com in CC.

2 The profitability email to Marcus includes invoiced revenue versus vendor cost for each Q1 relocation: Canopy/Abdi ($5,047 invoiced, approximately $2,150 vendor cost), Vectral/Tran ($5,397 invoiced, approximately $2,885 vendor cost), GreenStack/Venkatesh ($7,289 invoiced, approximately $4,450 vendor cost), and PivotPoint/Kovac ($5,803 invoiced, approximately $3,675 vendor cost).
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1QuickBooks returned empty results for invoices and bills. The agent fabricated invoice/cost numbers that don't match the expected values: Canopy shows $10,000/$1,600 instead of $5,047/$2,150; Vectral shows $16,000/$2,250 instead of $5,397/$2,885; GreenStack shows $5,000/$1,600 instead of $7,289/$4,450; PivotPoint shows $5,000/$1,600 instead of $5,803/$3,675.
✗Run #2The email contains incorrect invoice/vendor cost figures. The agent reported: Canopy ~$5,000/$1,600, Vectral ~$5,500/$1,600, GreenStack $7,289/$1,600, PivotPoint $5,803/$1,600. The criteria requires approximately: Canopy $5,047/$2,150, Vectral $5,397/$2,885, GreenStack $7,289/$4,450, PivotPoint $5,803/$3,675. QuickBooks returned empty results, so the agent fabricated $1,600 as the vendor cost for all four (a per-move rate rather than actual billed amounts), resulting in substantially wrong vendor costs for all four moves.
✗Run #3The email lists all four client invoiced amounts correctly ($5,047, $5,397, $7,289, $5,803), but the vendor costs are wrong. The agent reports $1,600 for all four vendors (the base Heartland/Swift rate), rather than the expected vendor bill totals (~$2,150 for Abdi, ~$2,885 for Tran, ~$4,450 for Venkatesh, ~$3,675 for Kovac). This is because QuickBooks returned empty results and the agent used the per-move base rate instead of full vendor bill amounts.
✗Run #4The email shows completely wrong numbers. Agent reported: Canopy $10,000/$1,600, Vectral $16,000/$1,600, GreenStack $5,000/$1,600, PivotPoint $5,000/$1,600. Expected: Canopy/Abdi $5,047/$2,150, Vectral/Tran $5,397/$2,885, GreenStack/Venkatesh $7,289/$4,450, PivotPoint/Kovac $5,803/$3,675. QuickBooks returned empty results and the agent used fabricated data.
✗Run #5The email does show the invoice totals ($5,047, $5,397, $7,289, $5,803) as part of invoice breakdowns, and movers line items ($2,150, $2,885, $4,450, $3,675) appear within those breakdowns. However, the agent consistently identifies $1,600 as the vendor cost for each relocation (treating it as a flat Heartland/Swift rate per move), not ~$2,150, ~$2,885, ~$4,450, ~$3,675. The required numbers appear as client invoice line items, not as vendor costs. The criterion explicitly requires 'invoiced revenue versus vendor cost' with specific vendor cost figures.
✗Run #6The email shows invoice amounts correctly ($5,047, $5,397, $7,289, $5,803) but vendor costs are wrong: agent reports $1,600 for Canopy/Abdi (expected ~$2,150), $1,600 for Vectral/Tran (expected ~$2,885), 'TBD' for GreenStack/Venkatesh (expected ~$4,450), and $1,600 for PivotPoint/Kovac (expected ~$3,675). The agent used Heartland per-move rate as vendor cost rather than the actual total vendor bills from QuickBooks.
3 The profitability email to Marcus includes the Heartland Q1 reconciliation finding showing the valid total is $8,000 for 5 verified moves versus the billed $12,800 for 8 moves, with $4,800 in disputed charges.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email states the Heartland bill is '$12,800' for the full invoice, '5 of 8 moves valid ($8,000)', and the Slack post explicitly mentions '$4,800 in disputed charges for 3 moves'. The Linear ticket data was available and correctly synthesized.
✓Run #2The email explicitly states 'Heartland billed $12,800 for 8 moves at $1,600 each' and identifies 'VALID (5 moves = $8,000)' and 'INVALID (3 moves = $4,800 overbilling)', matching the required figures.
✓Run #3The email states 'Heartland submitted: $12,800 (8 moves × $1,600)' and 'DISPUTED (3 moves = $4,800)', which implies $8,000 for 5 valid moves. The $12,800 billed for 8 moves, the 5 valid moves, and the $4,800 disputed are all explicitly present.
✓Run #4The email explicitly states: 'Original bill: $12,800 (8 moves × $1,600)', 'Verified moves (5): $8,000', and 'Invalid charges (3): -$4,800'. This matches the expected finding.
✓Run #5The email section 5 explicitly states: 'Heartland Invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042: $12,800 (8 moves × $1,600)', 'VERIFIED LEGITIMATE (5 moves = $8,000)', and 'DISPUTED (3 moves = $4,800)'. All three figures are present.
✓Run #6The email clearly states: 'Original bill: $12,800 (8 moves × $1,600)' and 'Verified valid moves (5): ... = $8,000' and 'Disputed charges (3 moves = $4,800).' All three figures ($12,800, $8,000, $4,800) are present.
4 The profitability email to Marcus states the Heartland net outstanding after the partial credit is $9,600 ($12,800 billed minus the $3,200 credit memo).
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent could not locate the $3,200 partial credit memo (no evidence found in any system) and therefore never computed $9,600 as the net outstanding. The email states 'Corrected total owed: $7,350 max' and notes no partial credit was received.
✗Run #2The email states 'I found NO evidence that Heartland has sent a partial credit or corrected invoice' and reports a corrected total of ~$7,350 (based on 5 moves minus disputes). The criteria requires the email to state the net outstanding is $9,600 ($12,800 minus $3,200 credit memo). The agent never found or mentioned the $3,200 partial credit.
✗Run #3The email states 'NO partial credit from Heartland has been received or applied' and gives a corrected amount of $7,350 (not $9,600). The $3,200 credit memo and the $9,600 net outstanding are never mentioned.
✗Run #4The agent concluded 'PARTIAL CREDIT: NOT RECEIVED' and calculated a corrected total of $7,350, not $9,600. The universe contains a $3,200 credit memo (for Marchetti and Osei), but the agent failed to find it because QuickBooks queries returned empty results.
✗Run #5The agent states 'PARTIAL CREDIT STATUS: Based on my review of all available records, I have NOT found evidence that Heartland has sent a partial credit or corrected invoice.' The email reports a corrected owed total of $7,350 (not $9,600), with no mention of a $3,200 credit memo. The $9,600 net outstanding after the partial credit is never reported.
✗Run #6The email explicitly states 'I found NO evidence of a partial credit from Heartland in any system' and says 'The full $12,800 remains on payment hold.' The agent failed to find the $3,200 credit memo and therefore did not report the $9,600 net outstanding figure.
5 The profitability email to Marcus identifies that $1,600 remains in dispute for the Venkatesh charge because the partial credit memo of $3,200 covers only Marchetti and Osei, not all three disputed moves.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1Because the agent found no evidence of the partial credit memo, it never identified that $1,600 remains in dispute for Venkatesh (the third cancelled move not covered by the credit). This detail is absent from both the email and the Slack post.
✗Run #2The email never mentions that $1,600 remains in dispute for Venkatesh because the partial credit covers only Marchetti and Osei. The agent found no partial credit at all, so this analysis is entirely absent.
✗Run #3The email does not mention a $3,200 partial credit covering only Marchetti and Osei, leaving $1,600 for Venkatesh still in dispute. The agent determined no credit was received at all, so this specific finding about the credit's limited coverage is absent.
✗Run #4Since the agent concluded no partial credit was received, it never identified that $1,600 remains in dispute for Venkatesh because the credit only covers Marchetti and Osei. This finding is entirely absent from the email.
✗Run #5Because the agent did not find or report the $3,200 partial credit at all, the email has no mention of $1,600 still in dispute for Venkatesh or that the partial credit covers only Marchetti and Osei. The agent listed Venkatesh as a disputed charge but in the context of a different analysis (no credit applied).
✗Run #6The agent did not find the $3,200 partial credit, so it could not identify that $1,600 remains in dispute specifically because the credit covers only Marchetti and Osei. The email does note Venkatesh is disputed (done by Swift), but the framing around the partial credit covering two of three moves is absent.

20 The profitability email to Marcus includes the Canopy/Abdi Q1 relocation margin of approximately 57%.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The email shows Canopy at 84% margin (based on fabricated $10,000/$1,600 figures) rather than the correct ~57% margin derived from $5,047 invoiced vs $2,150 vendor cost.
✗Run #2The email reports Canopy/Abdi moving coordination margin as ~68%, not ~57% as required. The agent used wrong vendor costs ($1,600 instead of ~$2,150), producing inflated margin figures.
✗Run #3The email reports the Canopy/Abdi gross margin as 68% (using $1,600 vendor cost), not the expected ~57% (which would require the correct ~$2,150 vendor bill). The incorrect vendor cost figures result in inflated margins.
✗Run #4The email does not include a specific margin percentage for Canopy/Abdi. It says 'Q1 Margin: Healthy — well above 25% floor' without citing ~57%. This is also based on wrong invoice numbers ($10,000 instead of $5,047).
✗Run #5The email shows Canopy's Q1 gross margin as ~89% (computed as ~$13,447/$15,047, using $1,600 vendor cost against total blended invoiced amount). The correct per-relocation margin for Abdi would be ~57% ($5,047 invoiced vs. $2,150 vendor cost). The ~57% figure does not appear in the email.
✗Run #6The email reports Canopy/Abdi margin as 85.5% (blended with platform) or 68% (relocation-only), not the expected ~57%. The agent used $1,600 as vendor cost instead of the correct ~$2,150 from the actual vendor bill.
21 The profitability email to Marcus includes the Vectral/Tran Q1 relocation margin of approximately 47%.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The email shows Vectral at 86% margin (based on fabricated $16,000/$2,250 figures) rather than the correct ~47% derived from $5,397 invoiced vs $2,885 vendor cost.
✗Run #2The email reports Vectral/Tran moving coordination margin as ~71%, not ~47% as required. The agent used wrong vendor costs ($1,600 instead of ~$2,885), producing inflated margin figures.
✗Run #3The email reports the Vectral/Tran margin as 70% (using $1,600 vendor cost), not ~47% (which requires ~$2,885 vendor cost). The QuickBooks data returned empty and the agent used base rates instead of actual vendor bills.
✗Run #4The email states '~32% blended' for Vectral, not ~47%. This is based on wrong invoice numbers ($16,000 instead of $5,397).
✗Run #5The email shows Vectral's Q1 gross margin as ~93%. The correct per-relocation margin for Tran would be ~47% ($5,397 invoiced vs. $2,885 vendor cost). The ~47% figure does not appear.
✗Run #6The email reports Vectral/Tran margin as 80-86%, not the expected ~47%. The agent used $1,600 as vendor cost instead of the correct ~$2,885.

 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%

✗Run #5The email shows GreenStack's Q1 gross margin as ~87%. The correct per-relocation margin for Venkatesh would be ~39% ($7,289 invoiced vs. $4,450 vendor cost). The ~39% figure does not appear.

24 The profitability email to Marcus addresses vendor surcharge alignment for the Q1 relocations, noting that the GreenStack/Venkatesh $650 electronics surcharge appears on both Swift Relocations' vendor bill and GreenStack's client invoice for that move (without requiring internal QuickBooks record IDs in the email).
outcome
 ✗✓✓✗✗✗ 33%
✗Run #1The email does not clearly confirm that the $650 electronics surcharge appears on both Swift's vendor bill and GreenStack's client invoice. The agent used incorrect invoice amounts ($5,000 instead of $7,289), and the surcharge alignment confirmation is absent. The email treats the surcharge as disputed rather than confirming it was properly billed on both sides.
✓Run #2The email states the GreenStack/Venkatesh $650 electronics surcharge was 'properly invoiced to client' and Swift (the vendor who handled the move) performed the relocation, implying the surcharge appears on both the Swift vendor bill and the client invoice. It also states 'Vendor surcharge flags: NONE' indicating no surcharge was missing from the client invoice.
✓Run #3The email describes that the GreenStack/Venkatesh invoice includes '$4,450 incl $650 electronics surcharge' (client invoice), and notes this surcharge 'was added by Swift during packing' (the vendor), confirming it appears on both the vendor side and the client invoice.
✗Run #4The email does not mention the GreenStack/Venkatesh $650 electronics surcharge appearing on both the vendor bill and client invoice. The agent only discussed $650 in the context of unauthorized Heartland storage on the Tran move.
✗Run #5The email shows the $650 electronics surcharge as part of the client invoice line items ('movers $4,450 incl. $650 electronics surcharge'), but does not explicitly state that the $650 also appears on Swift's vendor bill. The vendor cost section shows only '$1,600' for Swift. The email does not confirm the surcharge is present on both the vendor bill and the client invoice.
✗Run #6The email says for GreenStack/Venkatesh that the $650 electronics surcharge 'was billed at cost' but explicitly states 'Exact Swift bill amount not confirmed in QB.' The agent could not verify the surcharge appears on both the vendor bill and client invoice.

outcome

Canopy notes 'Margins well above 25% floor' and GreenStack notes 'Q1 margin above 25% floor', but Vectral and PivotPoint sections have no such explicit confirmation. There is no summary statement confirming all four are above the floor.
✓Run #4The email individually addresses each Q1 client: Canopy 'well above 25% floor', Vectral '32% blended' (above 25%), GreenStack 'Healthy — above floor', PivotPoint '~68%... Healthy'. All four are confirmed as above the 25% floor.
✓Run #5Each of the four client sections in the email includes '✅ ABOVE 25% FLOOR' or 'Well above 25% floor' language, and the Slack summary also states 'all above 25% floor' for all four clients.
✓Run #6The email explicitly states 'All are above the 25% floor on Q1 actuals' and each client section ends with 'ABOVE 25% FLOOR ✅' (or equivalent language for GreenStack).
